data.title: China SXT Pharmaceuticals Raises $760,000 in Registered Share Offering
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China SXT Pharmaceuticals ( (SXTC) ) has provided an update.

On April 7, 2026, China SXT Pharmaceuticals entered into a securities purchase agreement with investors to sell 2,000,000 Class A ordinary shares in a registered offering under its effective shelf registration. The shares, priced to yield expected gross proceeds of about $760,000, are being issued pursuant to a Form F-3 registration statement and related prospectus supplement filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

The offering is expected to close on April 9, 2026, subject to customary conditions, with the company planning to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes. The relatively modest equity raise provides incremental liquidity while slightly diluting existing shareholders, and it underscores China SXT’s continued reliance on U.S. capital markets to fund its operations and strategic flexibility.

More about China SXT Pharmaceuticals

China SXT Pharmaceuticals, Inc., based in Taizhou, Jiangsu, is a China-focused drugmaker whose business centers on the development and sale of pharmaceutical products. The company is listed in the U.S. as a foreign private issuer, giving it access to international capital markets to support its corporate and operational needs.

Average Trading Volume: 298,390

Sonoma Pharmaceuticals ( (SNOA) ) has provided an update.

On April 8, 2026, Sonoma Pharmaceuticals entered into a Manufacturing and Supply Agreement with Kenvue Brands LLC for the sale of its Microcyn® technology-based products in the United States. The contract becomes effective on October 24, 2025 and runs through March 2027, with the possibility of up to two additional one-year extensions upon mutual agreement.

The agreement provides Sonoma Pharmaceuticals with a defined multi-year framework for distributing its Microcyn® products through Kenvue’s U.S. network, potentially strengthening its commercial footprint and operational visibility in this market. The specified term and renewal options may offer greater planning certainty for both companies and could enhance Sonoma’s positioning in the competitive healthcare products sector.

Science Applications ( (SAIC) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 9, 2026, Science Applications International Corporation announced the appointment of technologist Paul Eremenko and retired Admiral Michael Rogers to its Board of Directors for a term beginning April 8, 2026 and running until the 2026 annual stockholders meeting. The Board expanded from eleven to thirteen members, and both appointees joined the Audit and Technology Committees, signaling a strategic emphasis on AI, aerospace innovation, cybersecurity, and national security expertise to support the company’s governance and long-term technology strategy.

Eremenko, CEO and Co-Founder of P-1 AI and former CTO at Airbus and United Technologies, brings deep experience in AI, aerospace R&D, and advanced engineering leadership across both private sector and government roles. Rogers, a former Director of the National Security Agency and Commander of U.S. Cyber Command, adds extensive military, cyber operations, and intelligence experience, with both new directors receiving standard pro-rated non-employee director compensation and no reportable related-party relationships with the company.

On April 9, 2026, Greenlane announced that its board had approved a $2 million share repurchase program following a recent reverse stock split, positioning the move as a way to support liquidity and signal confidence in the company’s asset base. The discretionary buyback, with no fixed end date, is framed as part of a broader capital allocation strategy aimed at enhancing shareholder value without obligating Greenlane to repurchase a set number of shares.

The company also reported that as of April 7, 2026 it held roughly 77.9 million BERA tokens, representing around 32% of the token’s circulating supply, and had deployed about 50 million units into validator infrastructure, alongside staking substantially all liquid holdings. data.title: WF Holding Announces 1-for-5 Reverse Share Split to Preserve Nasdaq Listing
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

WF Holding Limited ( (WFF) ) has issued an update.

On April 9, 2026, WF Holding Limited announced that its shareholders and board had approved a one-for-five reverse share split of its issued and unissued ordinary shares, effective for trading on a split-adjusted basis from April 13, 2026 under the unchanged Nasdaq ticker “WFF.” Following the corporate action, every five existing shares will be combined into one share, fractional entitlements will be rounded up, the par value will change to $0.00025 per share, and the company will have about 5,038,000 ordinary shares outstanding out of 200,000,000 authorized, a move aimed at lifting the share price to meet Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid requirement for continued listing.

The reverse split is designed to help WF Holding maintain its Nasdaq Capital Market listing, which can support liquidity, visibility and access to capital for the Malaysia-based FRP manufacturer and its investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (WFF) stock is a Hold with a $0.44 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on WF Holding Limited stock, see the WFF Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WFF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WFF is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by a strong, conservatively levered balance sheet and positive 2024 cash generation, but is held back by materially weakened 2024 profitability and a weak longer-term price trend with negative MACD. Valuation impact is limited due to missing P/E and dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on WFF stock, click here.

More about WF Holding Limited

WF Holding Limited is a Malaysia-based, ISO 9001:2015 certified manufacturer of fiberglass reinforced plastic products, including tanks, pipes, ducts and custom-made FRP solutions. With a track record of over 30 years, it focuses on supplying durable, high-performance FRP components and related services such as consultation, delivery, installation, repair and maintenance to reinforce critical industrial infrastructure.

data.description:

Soluna Holdings ( (SLNH) ) has issued an update.

Soluna Holdings, Inc., a Nasdaq-listed developer of green data centers, designs, builds, and operates digital infrastructure that turns surplus renewable energy into computing resources for Bitcoin mining, AI, and other intensive workloads. Its data centers are sited next to wind, solar, or hydro assets, and the firm markets MaestroOS software to enhance grid efficiency and deliver lower-cost, sustainable computing to partners and customers.

On April 9, 2026, Soluna reported March 2026 operational updates showing strong performance across its Bitcoin hosting sites, with Projects Dorothy 1 and 2 and Sophie operating at or near full capacity despite temporary transformer repairs at Dorothy 1A. The company said its Kati 1 hosting facility reached substantial completion ahead of schedule, advanced construction at K1B, continued development of AI-focused Project Kati 2 and Project Grace, and expanded its development pipeline beyond 4.3 GW, while naming KPMG as its new auditor and progressing land and power deals that underscore its push into larger-scale AI and high-performance computing infrastructure.

Management highlighted active interest from hyperscalers and neocloud providers in Project Kati 2, ongoing work on long-lead equipment procurement, and evaluation of on-site solar and gas generation options to support AI and HPC loads. Soluna also reported progress on multiple pipeline projects, including moving several developments outside ERCOT’s large flexible load study via sub-75 MW interconnection pathways and securing a land LOI for a Dorothy campus expansion, signaling a broader buildout strategy in both Bitcoin hosting and AI-focused data center capacity.

The most recent analyst rating on (SLNH) stock is a Hold with a $0.68 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Soluna Holdings stock, see the SLNH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SLNH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SLNH is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (large losses and heavy cash burn) and bearish technical trends (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). Positive corporate developments—especially the Briscoe acquisition supporting renewable-powered compute/AI ambitions—partially offset these risks, but added leverage and lack of profitable valuation support keep the overall score below average.

To see Spark’s full report on SLNH stock, click here.

More about Soluna Holdings

Soluna Holdings, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as SLNH, develops green data centers that convert surplus renewable energy into high-performance computing capacity. Its facilities are co-located with wind, solar, and hydro plants and focus on compute-intensive applications such as Bitcoin mining and generative AI, using proprietary software MaestroOS to optimize grid integration and deliver cost-effective, sustainable infrastructure.

The company positions itself at the intersection of renewable energy and digital infrastructure, targeting partnerships with Independent Power Producers and operating across multiple U.S. grid system operators. By aligning data center growth with clean power sources, Soluna aims to capitalize on rising demand for AI and high-performance computing while supporting grid stability and improving returns for energy and computing stakeholders.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from SUNation Energy ( (SUNE) ) is now available.

On April 9, 2026, SUNation Energy’s board launched a strategic review of alternatives intended to diversify operations and revenue streams while maximizing shareholder value. Options under consideration include a potential sale, strategic business combinations, acquisitions, asset divestitures, and further corporate structure optimization, with Maxim Group, Rimon PC, and Alliance Advisors engaged as key external advisers.

The process follows a transformation program discussed on March 18, 2026, under which SUNation streamlined operations, cut expenses, paid down high-cost debt, eliminated legacy financing instruments, and improved governance to fortify its balance sheet. Management, led by CEO Scott Maskin, says the stronger financial and operational footing provides a platform to explore transactions that could reshape the company’s growth trajectory and long-term positioning for shareholders, customers, and employees.

SUNation has not set a timetable for completing the review and has cautioned that it may not result in any specific deal or outcome. The company plans to provide further disclosure only if the board approves a particular strategic path or determines that additional communication is necessary, leaving investors to watch for potential consolidation moves or structural changes in the solar and energy services space.

The most recent analyst rating on (SUNE) stock is a Buy with a $2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SUNation Energy stock, see the SUNE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SUNE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SUNE is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by still-negative profitability despite improving revenue, leverage, and cash flow. Technicals are bullish but stretched, adding pullback risk. To see Spark’s full report on SUNE stock, click here.

More about SUNation Energy

SUNation Energy Inc. is a Nasdaq-listed provider of sustainable solar energy and backup power solutions serving residential, commercial, and municipal customers. The company designs, installs, finances, and services solar energy systems and related technologies, helping clients lower energy costs, boost energy independence, and transition to cleaner power sources.

Operating across residential and commercial markets, SUNation also offers battery storage and comprehensive energy services that position it within the broader clean energy and distributed generation sector. Its business model focuses on turnkey solutions, combining project design, installation, and ongoing service to build recurring customer relationships and long-term value.

The company emphasizes modernized corporate structures and governance to support growth and access to capital markets. By strengthening its balance sheet and streamlining operations, SUNation seeks to enhance competitiveness in a rapidly evolving solar and energy services industry, where scale, efficiency, and financing capabilities are key differentiators.

The latest update is out from OptimizeRx ( (OPRX) ).

On April 8, 2026, OptimizeRx announced the appointment of Mary Varghese Presti, Corporate Vice President and Chief Operating Officer of Microsoft’s Health & Life Sciences division, to its board as an independent director. Her addition is part of an ongoing effort to refresh and expand the board, signaling a focus on bringing seasoned healthcare and technology leadership to the company.

Varghese Presti brings more than 25 years of experience across healthcare, life sciences, and enterprise technology, including roles at Microsoft, Nuance, IBM Watson Health, athenahealth, and Pfizer. Her background in scaling AI-powered healthcare platforms, leading M&A integration, and driving operational and commercial transformation is expected to support OptimizeRx’s shift toward a more integrated, product-led, recurring-revenue model.

Executives said her expertise in deal strategy, platform scaling, and commercialization aligns with OptimizeRx’s strategy to expand its product portfolio and strengthen operational rigor. The appointment underscores the company’s push to accelerate growth and enhance its competitive position in the digital health and life sciences engagement market, with an eye to delivering greater value for customers and shareholders.

MEDIROM Healthcare Technologies ( (MRM) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 31, 2026, MEDIROM Healthcare Technologies Inc. signed a memorandum of understanding with Kufu Company Holdings Inc. to extend the maturity date of a deemed loan first agreed on January 30, 2026. The repayment deadline for principal and interest was pushed from March 31 to April 30, 2026, with all interest accrued through March 31 becoming immediately due, while all other terms of the original loan arrangements remained in force.

Under the updated terms, MEDIROM must repay principal and continuing interest by April 30, 2026, after which no further interest will accrue if repayment is completed on time. The extension offers the company short-term financing flexibility but underscores near-term liquidity obligations that stakeholders will watch closely as the new due date approaches.

Noah Holdings ( (NOAH) ) has issued an announcement.

Noah Holdings Limited reported in an April 9, 2026 filing that its issued ordinary share count on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange remained unchanged at 337,233,937 shares as of April 7, 2026, with no treasury shares outstanding. The disclosure also detailed a series of American depositary share repurchases conducted on the New York Stock Exchange between December 23, 2025 and January 27, 2026 under a June 12, 2025 shareholder mandate, with the repurchased shares earmarked for cancellation, signaling ongoing capital management efforts without altering the current issued share base.

The company’s Next Day Disclosure Return consolidates multiple small daily buybacks of ADSs, each representing ordinary shares, at prices mostly just above US$2 per ADS. While these repurchases are modest relative to Noah’s total equity, they indicate continued deployment of buyback authorization to enhance shareholder value and fine-tune its capital structure, yet with no immediate impact on the number of listed shares in Hong Kong as of early April 2026.
